page: Amazon Web Services i3.metal is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by two Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processors. It features 72 vCPUs across 36 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 7.11 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 15,200 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25% categories. However, the high core count compensates in parallel workloads, yielding average multi-core performance in stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth reads and writes also perform in the average tier, with uncached large-block reads reaching 121.2 GB/sec.

Given the massive 15.2 TB local NVMe SSD storage and 512 GB RAM, this bare-metal instance is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring dense local storage and high I/O throughput without virtualization overhead. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, NoSQL stores, Redis caching layers, and data-intensive processing tasks. The trade-off lies in its older Intel Xeon architecture, which underperforms in single-threaded tasks and modern vector-heavy computations, but offers stable, dedicated hardware resources for high-throughput storage applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.metal is a storage-optimized bare-metal server designed for high-throughput, data-intensive workloads. Powered by Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processors, it delivers 72 vCPUs (36 physical cores) on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 512 GB of DDR4 memory. The server's defining feature is its 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, complemented by a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. While single-core benchmarks show weak performance, multi-core and memory bandwidth tests fall into the average tier, reflecting its strength in parallel processing. This hardware profile makes the i3.metal highly efficient for large-scale databases, NoSQL deployments, and high-capacity caching layers where direct physical access to dense local storage is critical.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.metal is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 72 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database and data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3.metal is a bare-metal server with 72 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 15.2 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal instance delivering massive local NVMe SSD storage and high core density for data-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 72 vCPUs and 512 GB DDR4 RAM on dedicated bare-metal hardware
15,200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core parallel processing and average memory bandwidth
Optimized for large-scale databases, NoSQL, and caching layers
